meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Advanced SaaS Workflow for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to overcome SaaS knowledge gaps. A high-impact guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Bridge the SaaS knowledge gap by offloading diagnostic heavy-lifting to AI agents. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform complex, unfamiliar codebases into manageable, high-performing assets, ensuring your client projects remain on schedule without requiring deep internal system expertise.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on a high-stakes client deployment, and you're staring at a stack trace that looks like a foreign language. The pressure isn't just in the code; it's in the realization that your architectural knowledge has hit a wall. You don't have time to master the entire framework, yet the project must ship by Monday.

This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of tooling. Modern freelance engineering isn't about knowing every line of documentation—it's about orchestrating intelligence to solve constraints in real-time. We are going to turn your lack of deep SaaS knowledge into a competitive advantage by leveraging AI to act as your senior architect and debugger.

steps.title: Contextualize the Architecture
steps.description: Before touching code, map the dependency graph to understand how the system components interact.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this repository. Identify the core entry points, primary data flow, and the critical dependency chain. Create a visual architecture summary.
steps.claude_code_command: ls -R | claude ask 'Explain the high-level architecture and identify where the error likely originates.'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Isolate the failure point by forcing the AI to correlate logs with recent commit history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current production error logs with the last three commits. Identify the specific function responsible for the regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find the root cause of the current error by checking the logs in /var/log and cross-referencing with git diff.'

steps.title: Iterative Patch Development
steps.description: Draft a surgical fix that adheres to existing design patterns without introducing technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a patch for the identified bug. Ensure it follows existing functional programming patterns and include a unit test.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Apply the proposed patch, ensure type safety, and verify with a temporary test file.'

steps.title: Validation & Regression Testing
steps.description: Verify the fix doesn't break peripheral systems using automated simulation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests that specifically target the logic modified in the recent patch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the new test suite and ensure zero regressions across the codebase.'

steps.title: Documentation & Knowledge Capture
steps.description: Document the fix so you don't have to re-solve the same issue in future client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug, the fix, and the architectural lesson learned into a clean Markdown file for future reference.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Write a technical post-mortem and add it to the project's internal docs folder.'

pro_tips: Always force the AI to explain 'why' a fix works to prevent cargo-cult coding.
Use Antigravity to generate 'TODO' comments in code that the AI identifies as fragile for future refactoring.
When debugging, provide the AI with your package.json or go.mod file first to set the environment constraints.
Create a 'system prompt' file in your project root to keep the AI aligned with your specific coding style across sessions.
If the AI gets stuck, ask it to 'play devil's advocate' and find three reasons why its current solution might fail.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Code: https://promptingguide.ai
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project directory using the 'claude ask' command to verify your environment setup before attempting any refactors.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start mastering the architecture. You have the tools to ship faster than any solo developer in the industry. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
